differentials.name: macrocrania from obstructive hydrocephalus; seizures
differentials.raw: macrocrania from obstructive hydrocephalus; seizures (c)  adult pattern (> 1 year)

differentials.name: focal neurologic deficits (5%) ← steal of blood from surrounding structures ◊  Rarely diagnosed > 3 years of age
differentials.raw: focal neurologic deficits (5%) ← steal of blood from surrounding structures ◊  Rarely diagnosed > 3 years of age

context: =  central embryonic AVM of quadrigeminal plate cistern directly drains into a secondarily enlarged ectatic vein of Galen (aneurysm is a misnomer) Prevalence:   < 1% of all vascular brain malformations Cause:   arteriovenous fistula between primitive choroidal vessels and embryonic median prosencephalic vein of Markowski; fistula prevents involution of this embryonic vein + leads to development of the vein of Galen Anatomical types: = AV fistula fed by enlarged arterial branches → dilatation of vein of Galen + straight Type = angiomatous malformation involving basal ganglia + thalami ± midbrai
